_doc: Standard solutions / next steps / additional logs per problem_type. Keyed by the same problem_type strings declared in registry.json. Skills consume this via _shared/playbook.py.

solutions.title: Disable the most-recently-installed extension to rule out an extension crash
steps: Open edge://extensions and toggle off any extension installed in the last 7 days
Restart Edge and reproduce; re-enable one at a time
If a managed/force-installed extension is at fault, escalate to your IT admin

solutions.title: Update Edge to the latest stable version
steps: edge://settings/help
Restart after the update completes

next_steps.action: Enumerate recent Crashpad reports for a signature cluster
Check whether a managed policy restricts updates or features

additional_logs_needed.why: Need the actual minidumps to group by crash signature

solutions.title: Identify the hung renderer with Edge's Browser Task Manager (Shift+Esc)
steps: Sort by CPU; end the runaway process
If a specific site is implicated, test it in InPrivate with extensions off

next_steps.action: Check Crashpad in case the hang ended in a kill

additional_logs_needed.why: A hang may have terminated with a crash dump

solutions.title: Disable hardware acceleration if GPU process is implicated
steps: edge://settings/system → toggle off 'Use hardware acceleration when available'
Restart and reproduce

next_steps.action: Group crash reports by process_type and signature

additional_logs_needed.why: Renderer/GPU crash signature confirms whether driver vs. site is at fault

solutions.title: Disable Startup boost and any heavy extension that runs at launch
steps: edge://settings/system → toggle Startup boost
edge://extensions → disable extensions; re-enable one at a time

next_steps.action: Enumerate extensions and identify force-installed ones

solutions.title: Find the hot renderer via Browser Task Manager and isolate the site/extension
steps: Shift+Esc to open Edge's task manager
Sort by CPU; investigate top renderer's tab/extension

solutions.title: Enable Sleeping tabs and Efficiency mode
steps: edge://settings/system → enable 'Save resources with sleeping tabs'
Set efficiency mode = Always when on battery

solutions.title: Audit extensions for known memory hogs
steps: Run the edge_extensions skill
Disable extensions with very large background contexts

next_steps.action: List installed extensions and flag heavyweight ones

solutions.title: Confirm whether slowness is network-bound vs. CPU-bound
steps: Open edge://net-internals to capture a netlog
Compare a fast vs slow site to isolate proxy / DNS impact

additional_logs_needed.why: Captures per-request timings, redirects, proxy fallback

solutions.title: Validate DNS resolution and proxy configuration
steps: Resolve-DnsName <host>
Check edge://settings/system → 'Open your computer's proxy settings'

next_steps.action: Run DNS / proxy / cert sanity checks
Confirm no managed policy blocks the URL

additional_logs_needed.why: Pinpoints redirect chains and SSL handshake failures

solutions.title: Verify the system trusts the certificate chain
steps: certutil -verifystore Root <thumbprint>
If self-signed, import the issuing CA to Trusted Root

additional_logs_needed.why: Shows the exact handshake step that failed and the certificate chain Edge saw

solutions.title: Reset proxy settings to system default and retry
steps: netsh winhttp reset proxy
Restart Edge and reproduce

additional_logs_needed.why: Shows proxy resolution decisions and fallback attempts per request

solutions.title: Flush DNS and try a known-good resolver
steps: ipconfig /flushdns
Test with 1.1.1.1 / 8.8.8.8 to isolate local resolver

additional_logs_needed.why: HOST_RESOLVER_IMPL events expose which resolver step failed

solutions.title: Sign out, restart Edge, sign back in to refresh sync tokens
steps: edge://settings/profiles → sign out
Restart and sign in

next_steps.action: Check whether sync is restricted by managed policy

solutions.title: Identify the policy that blocks updates
steps: Run edge_policy to dump update-related policies
Coordinate with IT to lift or schedule the update

solutions.title: Disable suspect extensions one by one
steps: edge://extensions
Disable, restart, reproduce

solutions.title: Check ExtensionInstallBlocklist / ExtensionInstallForcelist policies
steps: Run edge_policy and search for Extension* keys

solutions.title: Surface the active policies to the user
steps: Open edge://policy for a live view
Or run the edge_policy skill for a JSON snapshot

solutions.title: Identify the policy controlling the feature
steps: Open edge://policy
Search the policy reference for the feature name

next_steps.action: Look up the question in the local Q&A knowledge base
